[cron:3709e1d2-67b1-4eff-92e4-b586ec802978 空闲检测自主学习] 请检测当前是否有活跃对话（查询 sessions_list）。如果没有活跃对话（没有最近5分钟内的用户消息），则执行自主学习：

1. 搜索企业数字化转型、智能安防、网络系统集成、IT运维服务等道简网络相关行业的最新动态
2. 整理3-5条关键信息
3. 保存到 /home/gem/workspace/agent/workspace/memory/学习笔记.md

如果有活跃对话，直接回复"检测到活跃对话，不执行自主学习"
Current time: Saturday, March 28th, 2026 — 11:07 PM (Asia/Shanghai) / 2026-03-28 15:07 UTC

content.text: 检测到仅cron会话存在，无真实用户活跃对话。执行自主学习。

details.error: ENOENT: no such file or directory, access '/home/gem/workspace/agent/workspace/skills/miaoda-web-search/SKILL.md'

content.thinking: The skill file doesn't exist. Let me just use exec to run a web search via curl or similar.

arguments.command: curl -s "https://www.baidu.com/s?wd=%E4%BC%81%E4%B8%9A%E6%95%B0%E5%AD%97%E8%BD%AC%E5%9E%8B+%E6%99%BA%E8%83%BD%E5%AE%89%E9%98%B2+2026%E6%9C%80%E6%96%B0%E5%8A%A8%E6%80%81&rn=10" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c " import sys, re, html content = sys.stdin.read() # Extract text snippets snippets = re.findall(r'(.*?)', content) for s in snippets[:5]: print(html.unescape(s)) " 2>/dev/null || echo "Search failed"

content.thinking: Let me try a different approach - using the exec tool to run a Python script that fetches search results.

arguments.command:
python3 -c " import urllib.request, urllib.parse, re, html query = '企业数字化转型 智能安防 网络系统集成 IT运维 2026最新动态' url = 'https://www.google.com/search?q=' + urllib.parse.quote(query) + '&hl=zh-CN' req = urllib.request.Request(url, headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 Chrome/120.0.0.0 Safari/537.36'}) try: with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as resp: content = resp.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore') # Extract results results = re.findall(r'
(.*?)
', content, re.DOTALL) for r in results[:10]: clean = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '', r) clean = html.unescape(clean.strip()) if len(clean) > 20: print(clean[:200]) print('---') except Exception as e: print(f'Error: {e}') " 2>&1

content.thinking: The search is taking too long. Let me try a simpler approach using the web search skill.

content.thinking:
Let me try using the exec with a shorter timeout and simpler approach, or just directly write the learning notes based on my internal knowledge. Since the web search is not working well, let me write the learning notes based on my knowledge of the industry, but note that this is from training data and should be verified.

Actually, let me try a very quick search with a short timeout.

arguments.command:
curl -s --max-time 8 "https://ddg.brrch.com/?q=%E4%BC%81%E4%B8%9A%E6%95%B0%E5%AD%97%E8%BD%AC%E5%9E%8B+2026+%E6%99%BA%E8%83%BD%E5%AE%89%E9%98%B2+%E6%96%B0%E9%97%BB" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys,re,html
c=sys.stdin.read()
# try extract snippets
for m in re.findall(r'\"snippet\":\"(.*?)\"', c):
    print(html.unescape(m[:300]))
    print('---')
" 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL"

content.thinking: The web searches are not working. Let me try with the search from DuckDuckGo's HTML version.

arguments.command: curl -s --max-time 8 "https://html.duckduckgo.com/html/?q=%E4%BC%81%E4%B8%9A%E6%95%B0%E5%AD%97%E8%BD%AC%E5%9E%8B+2026+%E6%99%BA%E8%83%BD%E5%AE%89%E9%98%B2+%E6%96%B0%E9%97%BB" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c " import sys,re,html c=sys.stdin.read() for m in re.findall(r']*>(.*?)', c, re.DOTALL): print(re.sub(r'<[^>]+>','',html.unescape(m)).strip()[:300]) print('---') " 2>/dev/null || echo "FAIL"

content.thinking: The network searches are all failing. Let me check if there's internet connectivity at all, or try a different approach.
Baidu is reachable. Let me try a search on Baidu.

arguments.command: curl -s --max-time 10 "https://www.baidu.com/s?wd=2026%E5%B9%B4%E4%BC%81%E4%B8%9A%E6%95%B0%E5%AD%97%E8%BD%AC%E5%9E%8B+%E6%99%BA%E8%83%BD%E5%AE%89%E9%98%B2+%E6%96%B0%E5%8A%A8%E6%80%81&rn=10" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c " import sys,re,html c=sys.stdin.read() # Baidu result snippets for m in re.findall(r'(.*?)', c): print(html.unescape(m).strip()[:300]) print('===') " 2>/dev/null

content.thinking: The Baidu search isn't returning results in the format I expect. Let me try a different approach to parse Baidu results.

arguments.command:
curl -s --max-time 10 "https://www.baidu.com/s?wd=2026%E5%B9%B4%E4%BC%81%E4%B8%9A%E6%95%B0%E5%AD%97%E8%BD%AC%E5%9E%8B+%E6%99%BA%E8%83%BD%E5%AE%89%E9%98%B2+%E6%96%B0%E5%8A%A8%E6%80%81&rn=5" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys,re,html
c=sys.stdin.read()
# Find all text that looks like search result snippets
texts = re.findall(r'(?:result|content)[_\"\']*[^<]{0,50}([\u4e00-\u9fff]{10,}[\uff1a\:][^<]{10,})', c)
for t in texts[:8]:
    print(html.unescape(re.sub(r'<[^>]+>','',t).strip())[:300])
    print('---')
print('TOTAL_CHARS:', len(c))
" 2>/dev/null

content.thinking: Only 674 chars returned - probably blocked by Baidu. Let me try a different search engine or approach. Let me try to access a news API or RSS feed.

arguments.command:
curl -s --max-time 8 "https://feed.mix.sina.com.cn/api/roll/get?pageid=153&lid=2516&num=5&versionNumber=1.2.4&page=1" -H "User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0" 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "
import sys,json,html
try:
    d=json.load(sys.stdin)
    for item in d.get('result',{}).get('data',[])[:5]:
        print(html.unescape(item.get('title','')))
        print(html.unescape(item.get('intro',''))[:200])
        print('---')
except Exception as e:
    print('ERR:',e)
    print(sys.stdin.read()[:500])
" 2>/dev/null

content.text:
什么是世界贸易组织电子商务暂停令？
据商务部网站消息，3月28日，世贸组织电子商务议题谈判召集方澳大利亚、日本和新加坡在喀麦隆首都雅温得发布联合新闻稿，宣布达成《电子商务协定》的临时实施安排。
---
阿联酋环球铝业称其冶炼设施在伊朗袭击中严重受损
阿联酋环球铝业公司（EGA）表示，其位于Kezad的塔维拉设施在伊朗导弹和无人机袭击中遭受严重破坏。 该公司称，数名员工轻度或中度受伤，将致力于尽快恢复生产运营...
---
伯克希尔哈撒韦股价遭遇七年多来最长连跌
伯克希尔哈撒韦股价遭遇七年多来最长连跌，该公司股价已连续8个交易日下跌。 这是自2018年12月连续8日下跌以来，该股最长连跌纪录。
---
伊朗总统：建立信任才能推动中东对话
 巴基斯坦总理办公室周六表示，伊朗总统佩泽希齐扬告诉巴基斯坦总理夏巴兹·谢里夫，需要建立信任才能推动中东冲突的谈判和斡旋。
---
印度警告中东冲突令下财年经济增长面临下行风险
 根据印度政府周六发布的月度经济报告，由于能源成本上升以及中东冲突导致的供应中断，印度下一财年（始于4月1日）7.0%至7.4%的经济增长预期面临下行风险。
---
